meaning: vt.约定,指定(时间,地点),任命,委任
examples: It made sense to appoint a banker to this job.
He promised to appoint an AIDS czar to deal with the disease.
After due consideration, we have decided to appoint Mr Davis to the job.
It also grants the parliament, which is controlled by Erdogan's party, the power to appoint several judges.
People will also be able to appoint a representative to confirm their wishes if they die.
